questions.question: Why does the writer declare sections, figures, and citations as data before any prose is written?
options: Because LaTeX requires a forward declaration
Because the structure is the contract: the harness can validate completeness before prose generation
Because the model needs a system prompt
Because BibTeX cannot resolve forward references
questions.explanation: Structure-first lets the harness validate uniqueness, citation coverage, and figure coverage before invoking the prose generator. The skeleton is the contract.

questions.question: What does the figure-injection step produce from an experiment manifest?
options: A PDF compiled from artifacts
A list of Figure records with stable ids and paths normalised to the paper directory
A model prompt with figure descriptions
A bibliography stub
questions.explanation: read_experiment_manifest returns Figure records. Ids are deterministic, paths are normalised, captions come from the manifest.

questions.question: Which validation failure raises PaperValidationError before any file is written?
options: An odd number of authors
A section that cites a key not declared in the bibliography
An abstract longer than 200 words
A figure path that does not exist on disk
questions.explanation: Missing bibliography keys, duplicate figure ids, empty title, empty abstract, and unknown figure refs all raise PaperValidationError. Existence on disk is not enforced because the LaTeX compiler enforces it later.

questions.question: Why is the prose generator a callable injected into PaperWriter rather than a method on it?
options: Because Python does not allow methods to take Paper as an argument
Because tests can swap a deterministic MockProseGenerator for a model call without touching the writer
Because LaTeX requires a callback
Because the dataclass cannot hold state
questions.explanation: The writer takes the prose generator as a dependency. The test substitutes MockProseGenerator. Production substitutes a model call. The writer is unchanged.

questions.question: What three files does PaperWriter.write emit on success?
options: paper.tex, paper.pdf, references.bib
paper.tex, references.bib, manifest.json
outline.json, paper.tex, figures.tar
manifest.json, prose.txt, paper.pdf
questions.explanation: paper.tex is the LaTeX source. references.bib is the bibliography. manifest.json is the structured artifact the critic loop reads next.

questions.question: Why is the manifest part of the writer's contract instead of just the LaTeX file?
options: Because LaTeX is too large to parse
Because downstream tooling (critics, dashboards) reads structured manifests, not LaTeX
Because manifest.json is the only file BibTeX accepts
Because LaTeX compilers reject untyped sections
questions.explanation: The manifest is a stable interface for downstream stages. Critics and dashboards inspect manifest.json. LaTeX is the human-readable artifact, not the machine interface.
